paper_title: A New Dynamic Modeling Approach to Predict Microbial Methane Generation and Consumption in Marine Sediments

methods.description: The process of selecting and utilizing data from the Janus database for modeling microbial methane generation and consumption in marine sediments at site 997, Leg 164 (Blake Ridge, North Atlantic U.S. margin). This site was chosen due to its high-resolution pore water chemistry data, presence of gas hydrate, and abundant microbial methane.

steps.description: Modeling was done using available data from Janus database (http://www-odp.tamu.edu/database/), which stores data for ODP.
steps.searchable_summary: access and retrieve data from Janus database for ODP

steps.description: Site 997 was selected to validate our numerical model results due to the availability of high-resolution pore water chemistry data, the presence of gas hydrate, and abundant methane of microbial origin.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model results using site 997 data
